results.summary: Transformed: Mana base improvement. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.97.
results.why: This pass improves the mana base by replacing a basic [[Island]] with [[Wayfarer's Bauble]], giving the deck a cheap way to ramp and fix early mana. In a Casual [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build, that small upgrade helps smooth land development without changing the deck’s core game plan.

raw.summary: Transformed: Mana base improvement. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.97.
raw.plan: This pass improves the mana base by replacing a basic [[Island]] with [[Wayfarer's Bauble]], giving the deck a cheap way to ramp and fix early mana. In a Casual [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build, that small upgrade helps smooth land development without changing the deck’s core game plan.
raw.why: This pass improves the mana base by replacing a basic [[Island]] with [[Wayfarer's Bauble]], giving the deck a cheap way to ramp and fix early mana. In a Casual [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] build, that small upgrade helps smooth land development without changing the deck’s core game plan.

added.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] turns early mana into reliable land ramp and color smoothing.

removed.island: A single [[Island]] was cut to make room for a more flexible mana-development piece.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.67.
results.why: This pass trims expensive, clunky pieces and replaces them with cheaper interaction, setup, and card selection to better match a [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] curve-tightening plan. The swaps lower the deck’s average mana value while keeping the game plan focused on efficient spellcasting and smoother early turns. Added cards also improve flexibility and consistency without pushing beyond casual power.

raw.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.67.
raw.plan: This pass trims expensive, clunky pieces and replaces them with cheaper interaction, setup, and card selection to better match a [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] curve-tightening plan. The swaps lower the deck’s average mana value while keeping the game plan focused on efficient spellcasting and smoother early turns. Added cards also improve flexibility and consistency without pushing beyond casual power.
raw.why: This pass trims expensive, clunky pieces and replaces them with cheaper interaction, setup, and card selection to better match a [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] curve-tightening plan. The swaps lower the deck’s average mana value while keeping the game plan focused on efficient spellcasting and smoother early turns. Added cards also improve flexibility and consistency without pushing beyond casual power.

added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] gives cheap, flexible instant-speed removal in place of slower top-end effects.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] improves early consistency with low-cost card selection.
added.sky diamond: [[Sky Diamond]] adds simple two-mana ramp that fits the lower curve.
added.snapcaster mage: [[Snapcaster Mage]] turns cheap spells in the graveyard into extra value at a low cost.
added.solve the equation: [[Solve the Equation]] efficiently tutors for the instant or sorcery you need.
added.thought scour: [[Thought Scour]] is a one-mana cantrip that fuels graveyard value and keeps cards flowing.

removed.commandeer: [[Commandeer]] is powerful but too costly and situational for a tighter casual curve.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was cut to make room for lower-cost, more reliable pieces.
removed.solemn simulacrum: [[Solemn Simulacrum]] is solid value, but four-mana ramp is slower than this pass wants.
removed.teferi, temporal pilgrim: [[Teferi, Temporal Pilgrim]] is a higher-end payoff that slows down the deck’s curve.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] is a very expensive finisher that does not support the streamlined plan well.
removed.twenty-toed toad: [[Twenty-Toed Toad]] is a costly alternate-angle threat that is less efficient than the new low-curve options.

warnings: Model output had 101 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Curve guard preserved the source land count because the mana base did not look broken.
Model output had 101 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Interaction guard removed additions that were not clear answers or protection.
results.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.92.
results.why: This pass adds cheap, flexible interaction so [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] can answer opposing threats without slowing its game plan. [[Pongify]], [[Rapid Hybridization]], and [[Reality Shift]] improve the deck's ability to respond at instant speed. The cuts remove lower-priority or narrower pieces to make room for cleaner answers at a casual power level.

raw.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.92.
raw.plan: This pass adds cheap, flexible interaction so [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] can answer opposing threats without slowing its game plan. [[Pongify]], [[Rapid Hybridization]], and [[Reality Shift]] improve the deck's ability to respond at instant speed. The cuts remove lower-priority or narrower pieces to make room for cleaner answers at a casual power level.
raw.why: This pass adds cheap, flexible interaction so [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] can answer opposing threats without slowing its game plan. [[Pongify]], [[Rapid Hybridization]], and [[Reality Shift]] improve the deck's ability to respond at instant speed. The cuts remove lower-priority or narrower pieces to make room for cleaner answers at a casual power level.

added.pongify: [[Pongify]] gives the deck a one-mana instant answer to problematic creatures.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] adds another efficient creature removal spell to increase interaction density.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] provides flexible instant-speed exile removal that answers creatures cleanly.

removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] is more situational and less reliable as interaction than the new removal.
removed.thought lash: [[Thought Lash]] does not support the interaction-focused pass as directly as the added answers.
removed.twenty-toed toad: [[Twenty-Toed Toad]] is a lower-priority payoff that was cut for more immediate disruption.

warnings: Model output had 101 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Interaction guard removed additions that were not clear answers or protection.

cautions: Budget route returned no one-for-one swaps.
results.summary: Budget swaps returned no safe cheaper pairs.

raw.summary: Budget swaps returned no safe cheaper pairs.

results.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.1.
results.why: This pass shifts [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] away from abrupt combo finishes and toward a slower, more interactive casual game. The added cards increase flexible draw, fair ramp, and answer density while making room for utility lands. Removing the all-in win pieces and trimming basic lands lowers the deck’s sharpness and supports a steadier table-friendly play pattern.

raw.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~3.1.
raw.plan: This pass shifts [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] away from abrupt combo finishes and toward a slower, more interactive casual game. The added cards increase flexible draw, fair ramp, and answer density while making room for utility lands. Removing the all-in win pieces and trimming basic lands lowers the deck’s sharpness and supports a steadier table-friendly play pattern.
raw.why: This pass shifts [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] away from abrupt combo finishes and toward a slower, more interactive casual game. The added cards increase flexible draw, fair ramp, and answer density while making room for utility lands. Removing the all-in win pieces and trimming basic lands lowers the deck’s sharpness and supports a steadier table-friendly play pattern.

added.aether spellbomb: [[Aether Spellbomb]] adds a simple utility piece that can draw a card or buy time.
added.aetherspouts: [[Aetherspouts]] is a big defensive reset that plays well in slower casual games.
added.behold the multiverse: [[Behold the Multiverse]] gives clean instant-speed card selection and draw.
added.bonder's ornament: [[Bonder's Ornament]] is fair ramp that also provides repeatable card draw later.
added.castle vantress: [[Castle Vantress]] turns a land slot into steady late-game card filtering.
added.command beacon: [[Command Beacon]] helps recast [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] without overcommitting mana.
added.commit // memory: [[Commit // Memory]] offers flexible interaction with a backup refill mode.
added.curse of the swine: [[Curse of the Swine]] is scalable creature removal that fits a slower pace.
added.decanter of endless water: [[Decanter of Endless Water]] adds stable ramp and removes hand-size pressure.
added.dream fracture: [[Dream Fracture]] is a softer counterspell that keeps games feeling fair.
added.evacuation: [[Evacuation]] gives the deck a clean instant-speed reset button.
added.fact or fiction: [[Fact or Fiction]] supplies strong but interactive card advantage.
added.gadwick, the wizened: [[Gadwick, the Wizened]] scales from early setup to late-game card draw.
added.into the roil: [[Into the Roil]] is flexible bounce that can replace itself.
added.jace's archivist: [[Jace's Archivist]] adds a repeatable, more casual wheel effect for card flow.
added.keep watch: [[Keep Watch]] can generate efficient draw in creature-heavy board states.
added.kindred discovery: [[Kindred Discovery]] rewards the deck’s creature type synergies with ongoing draw.
added.lonely sandbar: [[Lonely Sandbar]] is a land that can be cashed in when flooding.
added.memorial to genius: [[Memorial to Genius]] adds a utility land that becomes card draw later.
added.midnight clock: [[Midnight Clock]] is ramp early and a fair refill engine over time.
added.mindsplice apparatus: [[Mindsplice Apparatus]] supports a spell-heavy plan without relying on combos.
added.overflowing insight: [[Overflowing Insight]] provides a big splashy draw spell suited to casual play.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] is efficient creature interaction that keeps threatening boards in check.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] is another cheap answer to problematic creatures.
added.ravenform: [[Ravenform]] expands removal to hit creatures or artifacts.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] offers clean exile removal in blue.
added.remote isle: [[Remote Isle]] is another land slot that can cycle away when needed.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] gives flexible exile interaction for creatures or artifacts.
added.sea gate oracle: [[Sea Gate Oracle]] adds a modest body with card selection attached.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] smooths early draws and improves consistency.
added.sky diamond: [[Sky Diamond]] is straightforward ramp for a lower-pressure game plan.
added.terrain generator: [[Terrain Generator]] helps convert extra lands in hand into mana development.
added.thought collapse: [[Thought Collapse]] adds a serviceable counterspell with a small bonus effect.
added.thought courier: [[Thought Courier]] provides repeatable looting to smooth hands over time.
added.thought monitor: [[Thought Monitor]] is a value creature that helps refill the hand.
added.tome of legends: [[Tome of Legends]] turns commander casts and attacks into steady cards.
added.treasure cruise: [[Treasure Cruise]] is efficient card draw for a spell-dense deck.
added.unwind: [[Unwind]] is interactive protection that preserves mana for follow-up plays.
added.vizier of tumbling sands: [[Vizier of Tumbling Sands]] offers flexible cycling and small utility.
added.war room: [[War Room]] converts a land slot into repeatable card draw.
added.wash out: [[Wash Out]] is a broad tempo play that can reset opposing boards.
added.wavebreak hippocamp: [[Wavebreak Hippocamp]] rewards playing on opponents’ turns with extra cards.

removed.island: Extra [[Island]] copies were cut to make room for added utility lands and spells.
removed.jace, wielder of mysteries: [[Jace, Wielder of Mysteries]] was removed to lower the deck’s combo-finisher pressure.
removed.laboratory maniac: [[Laboratory Maniac]] was cut to move away from alternate-win combo lines.
removed.leveler: [[Leveler]] was removed because it mainly supports all-in self-mill combo kills.
removed.muddle the mixture: [[Muddle the Mixture]] was cut as a more specialized combo-tutoring piece.
removed.narset, parter of veils: [[Narset, Parter of Veils]] was removed to reduce harsher play patterns at casual tables.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was cut to free space for broader, lower-pressure value cards.
removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] was removed because it more often supports trickier combo turns than casual play.
removed.snap: [[Snap]] was cut to reduce explosive tempo and combo-adjacent sequencing.
removed.thought lash: [[Thought Lash]] was removed as another dedicated combo enabler.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] was cut because it can dominate games and enable repetitive loops.
removed.valley floodcaller: [[Valley Floodcaller]] was removed to make room for a more relaxed value-focused package.

results.summary: Transformed: More optimized. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.99.
results.why: This pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] more consistent and efficient at a casual optimized level. The additions improve card selection, interaction, tutoring, and mana utility while preserving a moderate budget. The result is a tighter blue spell package with better ways to find key effects and convert card flow into wins like [[Aetherflux Reservoir]] or huge draw spells.

raw.plan: This pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] more consistent and efficient at a casual optimized level. The additions improve card selection, interaction, tutoring, and mana utility while preserving a moderate budget. The result is a tighter blue spell package with better ways to find key effects and convert card flow into wins like [[Aetherflux Reservoir]] or huge draw spells.
raw.why: This pass trims excess basics and low-impact pieces to make [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] more consistent and efficient at a casual optimized level. The additions improve card selection, interaction, tutoring, and mana utility while preserving a moderate budget. The result is a tighter blue spell package with better ways to find key effects and convert card flow into wins like [[Aetherflux Reservoir]] or huge draw spells.

added.aetherflux reservoir: [[Aetherflux Reservoir]] gives the deck a clean payoff for chaining many cheap spells in one turn.
added.castle vantress: [[Castle Vantress]] upgrades a land slot into repeatable late-game card selection.
added.cephalid coliseum: [[Cephalid Coliseum]] adds a low-cost land that can reload your hand when needed.
added.cyclonic rift: [[Cyclonic Rift]] is efficient interaction that can also reset opposing boards to swing the game.
added.dispel: [[Dispel]] protects key plays and wins counter wars for just one mana.
added.emergence zone: [[Emergence Zone]] lets you deploy important spells at instant speed for better timing.
added.enter the infinite: [[Enter the Infinite]] provides an extreme top-end draw payoff for a spell-heavy deck.
added.everflowing chalice: [[Everflowing Chalice]] gives scalable ramp that fits multiple stages of the game.
added.fabricate: [[Fabricate]] tutors key artifacts like [[Skullclamp]], [[Midnight Clock]], or [[Aetherflux Reservoir]].
added.long-term plans: [[Long-Term Plans]] is a flexible tutor that sets up your next critical draw.
added.merchant scroll: [[Merchant Scroll]] efficiently finds important blue instants and interaction.
added.midnight clock: [[Midnight Clock]] adds ramp early and a strong hand refresh later.
added.mirrormade: [[Mirrormade]] copies your best artifact or enchantment for strong flexibility.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] is cheap creature removal that answers problematic threats efficiently.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] gives you another one-mana answer to dangerous creatures.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] answers creatures cleanly and exiles them.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] expands your cheap removal to hit creatures or artifacts.
added.sea gate restoration: [[Sea Gate Restoration]] is a spell-land that raises ceiling without costing a slot.
added.skullclamp: [[Skullclamp]] turns small creatures into efficient card advantage.
added.solve the equation: [[Solve the Equation]] improves consistency by tutoring the exact spell you need.
added.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] is scalable card draw and can also finish a player with enough mana.
added.swan song: [[Swan Song]] is premium one-mana protection against many key noncreature spells.
added.thought monitor: [[Thought Monitor]] provides efficient card draw when your artifact count is active.
added.tolaria west: [[Tolaria West]] is a land early and a tutor for utility lands late.
added.treasure cruise: [[Treasure Cruise]] is highly efficient card draw in a deck that fills the graveyard with spells.
added.wash away: [[Wash Away]] is cheap interaction that is especially strong against commanders.
added.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] helps smooth early development with reliable ramp.

removed.island: Extra [[Island]] copies were cut to free space for higher-impact spells and utility lands.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was removed for a more reliable and immediately useful effect.
removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] was cut because it is narrower than the upgraded interaction suite.

warnings: Model output had 118 cards after color filter; list trimmed to 100.
Budget guard removed newly added cards priced above about $35.
Budget guard kept estimated deck price around Moderate expectations.

results.summary: Transformed: General refinement. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.94.
results.why: This pass refocuses the list into a cleaner Modern-legal green-white creature shell with a clearer proactive plan. [[Collected Company]], [[Chord of Calling]], [[Rosie Cotton of South Lane]], and [[Scurry Oak]] raise consistency around creature-based pressure and combo potential. The mana base was also simplified with stronger untapped sources and a steadier land mix for smoother early turns.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~1.9.
results.why: This pass tightens the curve by adding more one- and two-mana plays, especially [[Elvish Mystic]], [[Llanowar Elves]], and efficient Golgari threats like [[Mosswood Dreadknight]]. The mana base is also streamlined toward black-green with cleaner untapped access from [[Llanowar Wastes]], [[Woodland Cemetery]], and [[Overgrown Tomb]]. Higher-variance off-plan cards and slower splash lands were cut to make early development more consistent.

results.summary: Transformed: More interaction. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.12.
results.why: This pass increases the deck's ability to interact on the stack and with creatures while keeping costs low for a Focused Modern build. [[Go for the Throat]] adds efficient removal, and [[Negate]] plus [[Spell Pierce]] give cheap answers to noncreature spells. Cutting basic lands and [[Tolls of War]] makes room for clearer, lower-cost interaction.

results.summary: Transformed: More casual. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.21.
results.why: This pass makes the list more casual by shifting out awkward or off-plan lands and a few lower-synergy spells for smoother, more focused fixing and cleaner role-players. [[Watery Grave]], [[Overgrown Tomb]], and the other land swaps help the deck cast its core colors more reliably without raising complexity. The added creatures and interaction also support a more straightforward game plan while keeping the deck in a focused, moderate-budget lane.

results.summary: Transformed: More optimized. Power Focused, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.21.
results.why: This pass tightens the deck into a more focused Golgari shell by upgrading the mana and replacing lower-impact, off-plan cards with efficient staples. [[Fatal Push]] and [[Cauldron Familiar]] improve early interaction and board presence, while [[Ygra, Eater of All]] adds a stronger payoff for the deck’s core game. The land changes make the deck faster and more consistent for a focused Modern build.
